mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth above 200k signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2015 payroll growth at 141.7M persons indicates sustained hiring momentum
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with stable labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of months with PAYEMS growth > 200k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero and inflation (CPIAUCSL) stays below 2.5% year-over-year, the real interest rate is deeply negative, incentivizing equity allocation over cash and bonds, producing sustained positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 FEDFUNDS at 0.12% and CPI at 235.98 index points indicate accommodative monetary stance
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with subdued inflation
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5% or t-stat < 1.5) when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2015 GDPC1 at 16264.1B (revised to 18666.6B) reflects ongoing economic expansion
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns following quarters with GDPC1 QoQ growth > 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprice recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2015 UNRATE at 5.5% (revised to 5.6%) shows stable labor market
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or shock event
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both series decline simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline > 1% AND HOUST month-over-month decline > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:May 2015 RSAFS at 444.9B and HOUST at 1135k units show resilient demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) of more than 0.5% signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2015 INDPRO at 105.10 (revised to 100.89) indicates stable industrial activity
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns following INDPRO declines > 0.5% are not significantly negative (mean return > -0.5% or t-stat > -1.5), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, the real interest rate becomes increasingly negative, but market expectations of imminent Fed tightening produce negative equity returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate rate hikes.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 inflation at 235.98 index and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% show divergence between price pressures and policy rate
regime_hint: monetary policy normalization cycle with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1.1M units annualized signals robust residential construction demand and forward-looking economic confidence, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through multiplier effects on employment and consumer spending.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2015 HOUST at 1135k units (revised to 1190k) indicates strong housing demand
regime_hint: post-crisis housing recovery with sustained demand
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns when HOUST > 1.1M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
